verdict_reason: This is one of the most-quoted Gita verses (yogaḥ karmasu kauśalam), and the engineering layer holds the line against the most common trivializations: 'effort', 'enthusiasm', 'flow', 'mindful presence', 'just doing your best'. The translation field explicitly forecloses each. The Sanskrit term kauśalam is preserved in the engineering body and anchored on Shankara's and Ramanuja's tight glosses. The dual-shedding insight (BOTH sukṛta and duṣkṛte) is preserved as a structural surprise — not flattened to 'don't be too proud or too anxious'. The artifact-density diagnostic is a strict, falsifiable test that prevents the verse from being read as motivational text. Pass.
